metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1618
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Saegusa et al investigate the subcellular localization of synaptotagmin 5 using PC12 cells stably expressing GFP-tagged syt-5. When neurite outgrowth is induced by NGF-treatment, syt-5 GFP colocalizes with syt-1 in dendrites (ICC). Subcellular fractionation by sucrose gradient centrifugation suggests enrichment of the protein on dense core vesicles. Following these observations, Saegusa et al perform immunoprecipitation of native syt-5 from mouse brain with an antibody raised against the C2A domain of the protein (Fig. 5A) and confirm its expression in the brain (see also Li et al, Nature 1995). Fractionation of mouse brain by sucrose density gradient centrifugation further supports the hypothesis of DCV association of the protein in the brain (Fig. 5 B and C). However, further validation is required.
Regarding neuronal subcelluar localization/DCV localization of synaptotagmin-5 (GenBank acc.# U20108), no further literature is available.

5/2/2017 Pim
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: Materials and Methods
Immunoprecipitation-Whole brain or pancreas from an adult ICR
female mouse was homogenized
